intro: Clerodendrum phyllomega, also known as Bitter Leaf, is a tropical plant species native to the Pacific Islands, characterized by its dark green, elliptical leaves and vibrant purple flowers. The leaves of Clerodendrum phyllomega have been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine to treat various ailments, and its extracts have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, offering potential health benefits for the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Clerodendrum phyllomega include clerodane diterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Polynesian medicine, Clerodendrum phyllomega is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal preparations, such as capsules and tinctures, to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
